::: {.r-fit-text}
## **🎯 Goal: Learn how to reduce computational time and code repetitions**
:::



::: {.cell}

:::



## Simulation Life Cycle

A typical simulation workflow includes the following steps.

![](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/dev/articles/images/simple_simulation.drawio.png){fig-align="center"}


## Performances: Factors to Consider

:::center-x

- Long loading and/or initialization
  - Model complexity
  - Scenario complexity (number of compounds)
- Long execution time
  - Model complexity
  - Scenario time span
  - Scenario complexity (number of administrations)
  - Solver settings
:::

# Single Simulation

## Load, Set, Run

![](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/dev/articles/images/simple_simulation.drawio.png){fig-align="center"}

## Load, Set, Run — Code


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ospsuite)

# Load the simulation
sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))

# Change Dose Parameter
doseParameter <-
  getParameter(
    path = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
    container = sim
  )

doseParameter$value <- toBaseUnit(doseParameter, 100, "mg")

# Run Simulation
results <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)[[1]]
```
:::




## About Single Simulations

- Easy to implement
- Linear process
- Runs only one simulation/scenario


# Iterative Simulations

## Sequential Runs

![](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/dev/articles/images/iterative_simulation.drawio.png){fig-align="center"}

## Sequential Runs — Code


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ospsuite)

# Load the simulation
sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))

# Change Dose Parameter
doseParameter <-
  getParameter(
    path = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
    container = sim
  )

# Simulation 1
doseParameter$value <- toBaseUnit(doseParameter, 100, "mg")
results_100mg <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)[[1]]

# Simulation 2
doseParameter$value <- toBaseUnit(doseParameter, 200, "mg")
results_200mg <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)[[1]]
```
:::



## About Iterative Simulations

- Simulations are run sequentially
- The simulation object is altered along the way
- Simulation is loaded only once
- Easy to implement and understand

::: center-x
Most useful for quick and easy run of simple and few simulations and scenarios.
:::

# Parallel Simulations

## Load Twice, Run in Parallel

![](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/dev/articles/images/parallel_simulation.drawio.png){fig-align="center"}

## Load Twice, Run in Parallel — Code


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
simulation_file <- system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
)

# Initialize simulation with dose set to 100mg
sim100 <- loadSimulation(simulation_file)

setParameterValuesByPath(
  parameterPaths = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
  simulation = sim100,
  value = toBaseUnit("Mass", 100, "mg")
)

# Initialize simulation with dose set to 200mg
sim200 <- loadSimulation(simulation_file)

setParameterValuesByPath(
  parameterPaths = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
  simulation = sim200,
  value = toBaseUnit("Mass", 200, "mg")
)

# Run simulations in parallel
results <- runSimulations(simulations = list(sim100, sim200))
```
:::


## Inspect Results

The output of `runSimulations()` is a named list of `SimulationResults` objects.

Each name of the list is a unique simulation identifier.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
print(results)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$`AZZb3ZGvbUirjmRPv9A_-g`
<SimulationResults>
  • Number of individuals: 1
For paths:
  • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)
  • Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound

$`1LgcK9m6VEmRpnvHgdix9w`
<SimulationResults>
  • Number of individuals: 1
For paths:
  • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)
  • Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound
```


:::
:::




## About Parallel Simulations

- Reduce simulation time (depending on the number of cores)
- Each simulation scenario is independent of the others
- One output: named list of `SimulationResults` objects.


::: center-x
Most useful when running several **different** simulations loaded from different files.
:::


# Batch Simulations

## Batch Workflow

![](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/dev/articles/images/batch_simulation.drawio.png){fig-align="center"}

## Freeze Structure, Vary Parameters

::: {.callout-important title="Define variable parameters at batch creation"}
Parameters or start values that can be varied later must be defined at the time of batch creation.
All other parameters are "frozen" and cannot be changed later.
:::


## Create and Run a Batch


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
parameterPaths <- c("Aciclovir|Lipophilicity", "Aciclovir|Permeability")

simBatch <- createSimulationBatch(
  simulation = sim,
  parametersOrPaths = parameterPaths
)

# Set parameters
# First value changes Lipophilicity, second Permeability
# Each call to addRunValues adds a new simulation run
# Each run gets a unique ID that is returned
simBatch$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(1, 2))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "0db0ec33-37b5-4371-b8f1-02479ad516ff"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
simBatch$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(3, 4))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "9e7cf9d5-800d-4a3c-aa38-b5088d78a527"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
simBatch$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(5, 6))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "8008da93-f98f-4238-b63b-92fd1c5db113"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
results <- runSimulationBatches(simBatch)
```
:::


## Nested Result List

`results` is a nested named list of `SimulationResults` objects. The names of the
first level are IDs of the simulation batches:


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
print(names(results))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "657c3f79-0c68-4cdb-87ad-ebd754e71a80"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
print(simBatch$id)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "657c3f79-0c68-4cdb-87ad-ebd754e71a80"
```


:::
:::


. . .

The names of the second level are the IDs of the runs added to that simulation batch:


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
print(names(results[[simBatch$id]]))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "0db0ec33-37b5-4371-b8f1-02479ad516ff"
[2] "9e7cf9d5-800d-4a3c-aa38-b5088d78a527"
[3] "8008da93-f98f-4238-b63b-92fd1c5db113"
```


:::
:::


## Run IDs and Clearing

::: {.callout-note}
Each run gets a unique ID which are used to access results in the result list


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
print(names(unlist(results)))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "657c3f79-0c68-4cdb-87ad-ebd754e71a80.0db0ec33-37b5-4371-b8f1-02479ad516ff"
[2] "657c3f79-0c68-4cdb-87ad-ebd754e71a80.9e7cf9d5-800d-4a3c-aa38-b5088d78a527"
[3] "657c3f79-0c68-4cdb-87ad-ebd754e71a80.8008da93-f98f-4238-b63b-92fd1c5db113"
```


:::
:::

:::

. . .

::: {.callout-note}
Run Values are cleared when calling `runSimulationBatches()`
:::


## About Simulation Batches


- Load and initialize a simulation only once,
- Undefined parameters cannot be changed later,
- Reduce simulation time (depending on the number of cores available),
- One output: named list of `SimulationResults` objects.

::: center-x
Most useful for running several scenarios of the **same** simulation.
:::


# Exercise

## Exercise Overview

Practice the three approaches on the same scenario (default 250 mg vs 100 mg dose):

- **Sequential.** Alter one simulation in place, run twice.
- **Parallel.** Load two simulations, run them together with `runSimulations()`.
- **Batch.** Freeze structure with `createSimulationBatch()`, vary dose via `addRunValues()`.
- **Two-parameter batch.** Vary dose and infusion time together.

## Sequential Baseline

In the second lecture, you have created two objects: `simExercise` and `simDose100`.

Here is a reminder of what it looked like:


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ospsuite)

# Simulation Setup
simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")

simExercise <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)
simDose100 <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)

Dose100 <- getParameter(
  "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
  simDose100
)
setParameterValues(Dose100, 100, units = "mg")

# Simulations are run
simExercise_res <- runSimulations(simulations = simExercise)
simDose100_res <- runSimulations(simulations = simDose100)
```
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

:::


. . .

- Loading time: 1.518 secs
- Simulation time: 2.506 secs.


## Parallel Exercise

Now is your turn! Try to do the same, but using the "parallel" approach.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")

simExercise <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)
simDose100 <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)

setParameterValuesByPath(
  parameterPaths = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
  simulation = simDose100,
  value = toBaseUnit("Mass", 100, "mg")
)

# We run the simulations in parallel
results <- runSimulations(simulations = list(simExercise, simDose100))
```
:::




::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

:::


. . .

- Loading time: 1.145 secs
- Simulation time: 1.49 secs.


## Get the results


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
results
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$`Lp22Lx45gkGBI-te1Z4lGQ`
<SimulationResults>
  • Number of individuals: 1
For paths:
  • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)
  • Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound

$YDiZv1oG9UaPE0jtm9JBSw
<SimulationResults>
  • Number of individuals: 1
For paths:
  • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)
  • Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound
```


:::
:::



. . .



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
results_250 <- results[[simExercise$id]]
results_100 <- results[[simDose100$id]]
```
:::

## Batch Exercise

Great, let's do the same, but with the "batch" function.



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
parameterPaths <- c(
  "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose"
)

sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)

simBatch <- createSimulationBatch(
  simulation = sim,
  parametersOrPaths = parameterPaths
)

# Careful about the units! The base unit here is in kg
simBatch$addRunValues(parameterValues = toBaseUnit("Mass", 250, "mg"))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "8c81dcf1-f753-4979-9f7d-a9becf393afa"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
simBatch$addRunValues(parameterValues = toBaseUnit("Mass", 100, "mg"))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "61a9ec04-bfcf-48a3-bf6d-8c56aaafab24"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
# We run the simulations
results <- runSimulationBatches(simBatch)
```
:::

## Two-Parameter Batch

Try to create one simulation with dose=250 mg and a 30 min infusion time, and another one with dose=100 mg and a 10 min infusion time.



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
parameterPaths <- c(
  "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
  "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Infusion time"
)
sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)
simBatch <- createSimulationBatch(
  simulation = sim,
  parametersOrPaths = parameterPaths
)

simBatch$addRunValues(
  parameterValues = c(
    toBaseUnit("Mass", 250, "mg"),
    toBaseUnit("Time", 30, "min")
  )
)
simBatch$addRunValues(
  parameterValues = c(
    toBaseUnit("Mass", 100, "mg"),
    toBaseUnit("Time", 10, "min")
  )
)

# We run the simulations
results <- runSimulationBatches(simBatch)
```
:::

- Simulation time: 1.486 secs.

<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
